gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: TWAP window collapse via low observation cardinality enables sandwich attack on swapTitanXForTorusAndBuild
candidate.claimed_root_cause: getTorusQuoteForTitanX falls back to oldestObservation when it is less than 900s; with pool cardinality=1 and attacker as last trader, oldestObservation collapses to ~12s, making amountOutMinimum near-zero
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker sandwiches the protocol swap in swapTitanXForTorusAndBuild, extracting up to ~91% of the TitanX value allocated to the current build interval

live_preconditions.name: TORUS/TITANX pool observationCardinality == 1
live_preconditions.observed_value: NOT IN LIVE CONTEXT — pool address not directly exposed; must be fetched via torusToken.titanXTorusPool() then IUniswapV3Pool(pool).slot0()

live_preconditions.name: TORUS/TITANX pool getOldestObservationSecondsAgo < 900 after attacker manipulation
live_preconditions.observed_value: NOT IN LIVE CONTEXT — depends on cardinality and last swap timestamp
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: Verify via fork test: after a large swap in block N, call OracleLibrary.getOldestObservationSecondsAgo(pool) in block N+1 and assert result < 900

live_preconditions.name: Protocol never calls increaseObservationCardinalityNext on the pool
live_preconditions.observed_value: Confirmed: no call to increaseObservationCardinalityNext found anywhere in src/contracts/src/**/*.sol

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Sets secondsAgo = min(900, oldestObservation); if oldestObservation is 12s (cardinality=1, attacker was last trader), TWAP window collapses to 12s, returning a manipulated near-spot price
amountOutMinimum = manipulated_quote * 0.9; with a 10x price manipulation this is ~9% of fair value, providing effectively no slippage protection
Public nonReentrant function callable by any address; calls _swapTitanXForTorus(half, _deadline) with protocol-owned TitanX; attacker triggers this as the victim transaction in the sandwich

With observationCardinality=1, (observationIndex+1)%1 == 0, so it reads observations[0] which is the single slot; secondsAgo = block.timestamp - lastObservationTimestamp, collapsing to ~12s after attacker's block-N swap
require(secondsAgo != 0) is the only guard; it does NOT prevent a 12-second window, so the manipulated tick is accepted
INCENTIVE_FEE_BUILD=150 BPS (1.5%); attacker's entitled gain from calling the function is only 1.5% of interval allocation; sandwich profit is the excess beyond this entitlement

attacker_path.actor: Any unprivileged EOA with capital to execute a large swap in the TORUS/TITANX Uniswap V3 pool
attacker_inputs: Block N: large swap in TORUS/TITANX pool (TORUS→TITANX direction) to push tick to very low TORUS price, writing a new observation at T_N
Block N+1: call swapTitanXForTorusAndBuild(deadline) as the trigger transaction
Block N+1: MEV sandwich — front-run the protocol swap (buy TORUS cheap) and back-run it (sell TORUS at higher price after protocol's swap moves price)
call_sequence: Block N: attacker → IUniswapV3Pool(titanXTorusPool).swap(...) — manipulates tick, writes observation at T_N with very low TORUS price
→ getTorusQuoteForTitanX(half): oldestObservation = block.timestamp(N+1) - T_N ≈ 12; secondsAgo = min(900,12) = 12; returns near-zero manipulated quote
→ ISwapRouter.exactInput executes with near-zero floor — attacker's front-run position profits from price impact
→ _addLiquidityBuild(remainder, torusPurchased, deadline): adds liquidity with depressed torusPurchased amount
state_before: Pool cardinality = 1 (assumed default, unconfirmed from live context)
state_after: Protocol receives far fewer TORUS tokens than fair market value for the half-TitanX swapped
Attacker captures price impact delta via MEV sandwich
Attacker also receives INCENTIVE_FEE_BUILD (1.5%) of interval allocation as the caller

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: TitanX allocated to the current build interval (from totalTitanXForBuild = 5191597325509844865659654507)
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: MEV sandwich profit: up to ~91% of the fair TORUS value of the half-TitanX swapped, captured as price impact; plus 1.5% incentive fee as caller
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol receives TORUS tokens worth ~9% of fair value for the TitanX spent; the remaining ~91% of value is extracted by the sandwich attacker
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker is entitled only to the 1.5% INCENTIVE_FEE_BUILD for triggering the interval. The sandwich profit (up to ~91% of swap value) is extracted from protocol-owned TitanX that was allocated for liquidity building, not for attacker compensation

setup: Assert IUniswapV3Pool(pool).slot0() observationCardinality == 1; if > 1, reject finding
Trigger buildTitanXIntervalUpdate to ensure a fresh interval is available with non-zero amountAllocated
transaction_sequence: Execute large TORUS→TITANX swap in pool to push tick to very low TORUS price (writes observation at T_N)
vm.roll(block.number + 1); vm.warp(block.timestamp + 12); — advance to block N+1
Capture torusPurchased from emitted BuyAndBuild(titanXUsed, torusPurchased, caller) event
expected_assertions: assertLt(torusPurchased, fairQuote * 90 / 100, 'sandwich extracted value: torusPurchased below 90% of fair quote')
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If pool observationCardinality > 1 AND getOldestObservationSecondsAgo(pool) >= 900 holds after the manipulation attempt, the TWAP window does not collapse and the finding must be REJECTED. Also reject if torusPurchased >= fairQuote * 90 / 100.
